description: Central registry of forbidden control patterns. Context-aware: scripts/code are strict; docs allow negated boundary phrases. Not an automated runtime enforcer — wording and pattern guidance source of truth.

app_apply.description: Patterns that would indicate direct application of app state by non-app actors

external_send.description: Patterns that would indicate sending data to external systems without authorization

hidden_tool_execution.description: Patterns that would indicate undeclared tool execution

public_network_bind.description: Patterns that would bind to public/non-localhost addresses

provider_model_execution.description: Patterns that would execute live provider/model calls without receipts

windows_service_install.description: Patterns that would create or install Windows services

tray_launch.description: Patterns that would launch a system tray application

installer_creation.description: Patterns that would create real installer artifacts

code_signing.description: Patterns that would perform actual code signing

registry_mutation.description: Patterns that would mutate Windows registry

task_scheduler_mutation.description: Patterns that would create or mutate Windows Task Scheduler entries

admin_elevation.description: Patterns that would request or assume administrative elevation

destructive_cleanup.description: Patterns that would perform destructive or irreversible cleanup without explicit user authorization

credential_exfiltration.description: Patterns that would extract or expose credentials

unredacted_support_bundle.description: Patterns that would emit unredacted sensitive data in support bundles

context_rules.docs: allow negated boundary phrases; forbid positive authority patterns
context_rules.examples: safe placeholders only; no real credentials; no real external targets
context_rules.reports: allow negated boundary phrases; allow classification discussion

false_positive_prevention.note: Docs and reports must be allowed to discuss forbidden patterns in negated context. Rules must not flag phrases like 'no app apply by agent' or 'not_production_readiness'. Only flag positive authority claims in wrong context.
